commit.message:
feat(matter): resolve SBMD endpoints by device type matching (#174)

The original method used to locate the Matter device endpoint to use for
a resource originated from the method used by Zigbee. Zigbee doesn't
have the device types and parts list attributes which are much better
suited to locate endpoints hosting desired functionality. Our drivers
are for specific device types (on/off light, dimmable light, etc). This
maps directly to the device types attribute on Matter endpoints and is
more reliable than just looking for an endpoint with a target cluster as
before.

- Add endpoint map (sbmdEndpointMap) to MatterDevice, built during
AddDevice() by matching device type lists against driver's
matterMeta.deviceTypes
- Add ResolveEndpointMap() and GetEndpointForSbmdIndex() methods
- Update BindResourceReadInfo, BindWriteInfo, BindExecuteInfo, and
BindResourceEventInfo to accept optional sbmdEndpointIndex parameter
- Pre-resolve Matter endpoint IDs at bind time; device-level resources
fall back to GetEndpointForCluster()
- Store resolvedEndpointId on write/execute bindings for use at
invocation time
- Update SpecBasedMatterDeviceDriver to pass endpoint index for
endpoint-level resources and std::nullopt for device-level resources
- Add MatterDeviceEndpointMapTest with 27 tests covering endpoint map
resolution, index lookup, and all four binding methods
- Add device-type-endpoint-resolution spec to openspec/specs
- Archive endpoints-from-device-types change

Refs: BARTON-358

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: widen percentage range to 1-100 across models and simplify fallback

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100
- Summary model: same range update
- _get_percentage: replace random 30-80 fallback with deterministic
  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

Triggers on every push to main. Installs fastapi-cli, restores the
auth token from the FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH GitHub secret, then runs
`fastapi deploy . --no-wait` which uses the app_id from .fastapicloud/cloud.json.

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine
2. Add it as a GitHub secret named FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH in the repo settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: fix deploy workflow to use uv + PAT token auth

- Use uv sync to install deps (matches project toolchain)
- Authenticate with `fastapi cloud login --token` using PAT
- Remove incorrect auth.json approach
- Run fastapi via `uv run` to use project venv

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: recreate .fastapicloud/cloud.json in CI before deploy

Since .fastapicloud is gitignored, the folder won't exist after
checkout. Recreate cloud.json from GitHub secrets so `fastapi deploy .`
can find the app_id and team_id.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply backdrop intensity and transparency

macOS: apply visuals.backdrop_intensity to the NSVisualEffectView alpha (clamped) so backdrop intensity affects the effect view.

Windows: honor visuals.backdrop_intensity by computing an alpha-packed accent color, toggle WS_EX_LAYERED for transparent windows, call SetLayeredWindowAttributes and DwmExtendFrameIntoClientArea when needed, and set the AccentPolicy accordingly (enable blur for transparent or non-none backdrops, set gradient_color based on intensity, and set accent_flags depending on DWM attribute result).

Tests: update expected backdrop_intensity in a unit test from 4.0/1.0 to 0.25 and assert a platform op is emitted. These changes ensure backdrop intensity and transparency settings are respected across platforms.

perf: parallelize programs + staff DB queries in provider dashboard mount

Both ProgramCatalog.list_programs_for_provider/1 and Provider.list_staff_members/1
are independent DB queries fired sequentially in the dashboard mount. Running them
concurrently with Task.async/await saves ~1 DB round-trip worth of wall time
(typically 5–15ms) per provider dashboard page load.

commit.message:
Implement Entity Source Generator

Implement the 5 core files of the entity source generator:

- EntityGenerator.cs: Main entry using ForAttributeWithMetadataName to
  discover [Entity] classes, parse columns/indexes/DetailOf attributes,
  map CLR types to PostgreSQL types, and invoke all 4 emitters

- DalEmitter.cs: Generates CREATE TABLE DDL with proper column types,
  NOT NULL constraints, PRIMARY KEY, PARTITION BY LIST for tenant tables,
  audit table DDL, and CREATE INDEX statements

- CrudEmitter.cs: Generates INSERT/SELECT/DELETE SQL constants,
  BuildInsertParams helper with proper NpgsqlParameter/NpgsqlDbType
  mapping, and ReadEntity method with correct reader methods

- AuditCrudEmitter.cs: Generates audit table name constant and
  DiffAuditSnapshots method using System.Text.Json.JsonDocument for
  NativeAOT-safe snapshot comparison

- DescEmitter.cs: Generates table name/tenant constants, Fields nested
  class with column name constants, DictFieldMetas dictionary with
  YTFieldMeta entries, and GetFieldMeta lookup method

Also adds <Compile Remove="Generated/**" /> to the sample project to
prevent double-compilation of emitted files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Repo Assist] Add encoding parameter to Frame.ReadCsv path/stream overloads (Closes #346) (#617)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Adds an optional `encoding` parameter to `Frame.ReadCsv` overloads that
accept a file path or `Stream`, so users can read CSV files with
non-UTF-8 encodings without having to construct a `StreamReader`
manually.

Closes #346.

## Root Cause

The path- and stream-based `ReadCsv` overloads always constructed `new
StreamReader(path)` / `new StreamReader(stream)` with no encoding
argument, defaulting to UTF-8. The `TextReader` overload was already
encoding-agnostic, but it required users to build their own
`StreamReader` — a workaround that was not obvious.

## Changes

- `src/Deedle/FrameExtensions.fs`
- F# `ReadCsv(path, ..., ?encoding)` — passes encoding to `StreamReader`
when provided
  - F# `ReadCsv<'R>(path, indexCol, ..., ?encoding)` — same
  - F# `ReadCsv(stream, ..., ?encoding)` — same
- C# `ReadCsv(location, ..., encoding)` — same (optional `[(Optional)]`
parameter, `null` = default)
  - C# `ReadCsv(stream, ..., encoding)` — same
  - Added `open System.Text` for convenient `Encoding` usage
- `tests/Deedle.Tests/Frame.fs`
- Added two regression tests for Latin-1 encoding via path and stream
overloads

## Usage

**F#:**
```fsharp
open System.Text

// Read a Latin-1 encoded CSV
let df = Frame.ReadCsv("file.csv", encoding = Encoding.GetEncoding("iso-8859-1"))

// Read a Windows-1252 CSV from a stream
use stream = File.OpenRead("data.csv")
let df2 = Frame.ReadCsv(stream, encoding = Encoding.GetEncoding(1252))
```

**C#:**
```csharp
using System.Text;

var df = Frame.ReadCsv("file.csv", encoding: Encoding.GetEncoding("iso-8859-1"));
```

## Test Status

✅ All 496 tests pass (494 existing + 2 new encoding tests).

[Repo Assist] Add tests for previously untested Frame and Series module functions (#613)

🤖 *Repo Assist — automated AI improvement (Task 9: Testing
Improvements).*

## Summary

Adds test coverage for **14 Frame and Series module functions** that
existed in the library but had no tests.

### Frame functions now covered

| Function | What it does |
|---|---|
| `Frame.mapRows` | Map `(key, row) -> value` across all rows, returning
a `Series` |
| `Frame.mapRowValues` | Map `row -> value` across all rows (without
key) |
| `Frame.mapRowKeys` | Transform row keys with a function |
| `Frame.filterCols` | Filter columns by `(col-key, col-series) -> bool`
|
| `Frame.filterColValues` | Filter columns by `col-series -> bool` |
| `Frame.mapCols` | Map columns by `(col-key, col-series) -> col-series`
|
| `Frame.mapColValues` | Map column data with identity (verifying
round-trip) |

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add Stats.describe for Frame (pandas-style per-column summary statistics) (#612)

🤖 *Repo Assist — automated AI improvement.*

Closes #398

## Summary

Implements `Stats.describe` for `Frame<'R,'C>` (and corresponding
`FrameStatsExtensions.Describe` for C#), a long-requested (#398)
pandas-style summary statistics method.

### What it does

Calling `Stats.describe frame` produces a `Frame(string, 'C)` with one
column per numeric column of the input frame, and these row keys:

| Key | Meaning |
|-----|---------|
| `unique` | number of unique values |
| `mean` | arithmetic mean |
| `std` | sample standard deviation |
| `min` | minimum |
| `0.25` | 25th percentile |
| `0.5` | median |
| `0.75` | 75th percentile |
| `max` | maximum |

Only columns convertible to `float` are included (via
`frame.GetColumns(float)()`). Non-numeric columns are silently dropped,
matching the behaviour of pandas.

### Usage (F#)

```fsharp
let df = Frame.ofColumns [ "Open", openSeries; "Close", closeSeries ]
let summary = Stats.describe df
// summary is a Frame(string, string)
// summary.["Open", "mean"]  -> mean of Open column
// summary.["Close", "min"]  -> min of Close column
```

### Usage (C#)

```csharp
var summary = df.Describe();
// summary["Open", "mean"]  -> mean of Open column
```

## Root cause / approach

`Stats.describe` already exists for `Series<'K,'V>` and returns a
`Series(string, float)`. The frame variant simply maps this function
over all numeric columns via `frame.GetColumns(float)() |> Series.map
(fun _ s -> Stats.describe s)` and assembles the result into a frame
using `FrameUtils.fromColumns`.

method |
